summary: Brainstorm a full feature roadmap for Dust Busters from multiple product lenses, deep-design the Uber-style live cleaner-map matching experience, then synthesize a prioritized plan
logs: parallel[5] failed: agent({schema}): StructuredOutput retry cap (5) exceeded — 5 failed calls with no valid output

synthesis.executiveSummary: I merged ~70 ideas from six lenses plus the Uber-map UX design and tech plan into one prioritized, de-duplicated roadmap, validated against the real code. Headline finding: several customer-facing promises are currently unbacked, and those are the true production blockers, not the map. Confirmed in-repo: the reviews hasReview query filters reviews.reviewer_id, a column that does not exist (0006), so it silently fails; no scheduler/cron exists anywhere, so offer_state='expired' is never set and bookings.deposit_deadline (0008) is never enforced; accept_offer has no double-booking guard (a cleaner can accept two overlapping jobs); the 15% fee plus Friday direct deposit is a display-only constant with no payout rail; the ID-verified / background-checked badge is an unaudited admin click; cancel_booking does no timing check or refund despite the 24h policy copy; open_dispute is customer-only; customer_favorites exists with full RLS but has zero references in src/; notifications are in-app only. P0 fixes these. P1 is the flagship Uber-style live cleaner map, reconciled into one buildable spec (react-leaflet plus OSM primary with stylized-SVG fallback, one fuzzing SECURITY DEFINER RPC get_booking_matching returning live counts plus fuzzed pins plus winner, seeded by cleaner_id and booking_id, no fake GPS/ETA). P2/P3 sequence the LTV, supply, ops, and growth levers. Full detail in docs/ROADMAP.md and docs/specs/uber-cleaner-map.md.

roadmap.name: Fix broken reviews query + finalize reviews schema
roadmap.whatItIs: Add reviewer_id to the reviews table (or correct the page query) so the duplicate-review check works.
roadmap.why: Confirmed bug: src/app/bookings/[id]/page.tsx line 104 filters reviews.reviewer_id, but the reviews table (0006_reviews.sql) has no such column, so hasReview silently fails and the review prompt can re-appear after a review. Also unblocks two-way reviews.

roadmap.name: Dispatch scheduler heartbeat (dispatch_tick plus cron)
roadmap.whatItIs: One SECURITY DEFINER dispatch_tick() run every 30 to 60s (Supabase pg_cron or Vercel cron to a service-role route) that expires stale rung offers, enforces deposit_deadline, expires broadcast_expires_at to no_cleaner_found, and fires reminder/SLA hooks.
roadmap.why: No scheduler exists anywhere (grep-confirmed): offer_state='expired' is never set, deposit_deadline (0008) is never enforced so an accepted-but-unpaid booking pins a cleaner forever, and the jobs UI claims an offer window that does not exist. The shared time-source half the roadmap depends on.
roadmap.dependencies: none (enables map TTL, recurring, reminders, abandoned-deposit recovery, SLA)

roadmap.name: Schedule-conflict / double-booking guard
roadmap.whatItIs: In request_booking offer fan-out and inside accept_offer, exclude/reject cleaners whose existing active job time window overlaps the new booking plus a travel buffer; add a partial index on (cleaner_id, scheduled_at).
roadmap.why: accept_offer (0003) only checks booking status plus offer existence, so a cleaner can accept two overlapping jobs today, a guaranteed no-show. Real correctness bug at the core of matching.

roadmap.name: Cleaner self-serve Online/Offline toggle
roadmap.whatItIs: Add cleaner_details.accepting_jobs bool (distinct from admin-only active) and append 'and cd.accepting_jobs' to the dispatch WHERE clause.
roadmap.why: Today the only on/off is admin-controlled active. A cleaner who is sick/booked/on vacation cannot pause and gets spammed with offers they must decline, table-stakes gig retention, and a one-line dispatch change.

roadmap.name: Enforced cancellation windows + automatic Stripe refunds
roadmap.whatItIs: Make cancel_booking timing-aware: more than 24h before gives a full deposit refund via the existing service-role refund path; within 24h forfeit or partial; record the outcome and show the exact refund at cancel time.
roadmap.why: cancel_booking (0008) just sets status='cancelled', it never checks timing or refunds, yet the booking page renders a 24h-refund policy. Direct money plus chargeback liability at launch.

roadmap.name: Formalize platform commission in data + honest money copy
roadmap.whatItIs: Add settings.commission_percent; compute and persist platform_fee plus cleaner_payout per booking in request_booking; surface real platform revenue in admin; correct the earnings page copy until Connect ships.
roadmap.why: The marketplace records ZERO revenue: the 15% cut is a display-only constant (cleaner/earnings/page.tsx) and the page claims automated Friday direct deposit that does not exist. Precondition for all monetization and for not misleading cleaners.

roadmap.name: Real ID verification (document upload + admin review)
roadmap.whatItIs: Private Supabase Storage bucket for govt ID plus selfie captured at onboarding; admin reviews docs before flipping id_verified. Recommended upgrade: Stripe Identity VerificationSession plus webhook.
roadmap.why: id_verified is an unaudited admin boolean with no document capture, while /book claims cleaners are background-checked and the booking page shows a green ID-verified badge. Shipping a false trust claim about strangers entering homes is a real liability.
roadmap.dependencies: Supabase Storage (or Stripe Identity, vendor already wired)

roadmap.name: Transactional email/SMS on money + match events
roadmap.whatItIs: Extend the single createNotification() choke point to also send email (Resend) and SMS (Twilio; profiles.phone exists) for cleaner-found-pay-deposit, job-complete-pay-balance, leave-a-review; add channel prefs and unsubscribe.
roadmap.why: Notifications are in-app only, but the entire deposit-to-balance cash flow depends on the customer acting on prompts they only see when on-site. Out-of-app nudges directly lift conversion and cash collection, and seed all lifecycle/win-back.

roadmap.name: Cleaner-side issue reporting (two-way disputes + SOS hook)
roadmap.whatItIs: Generalize open_dispute so the assigned cleaner can raise issues (customer no-show, unsafe home, harassment, access denied) into the same admin queue; add a Report-a-problem action on the cleaner job page.
roadmap.why: open_dispute (0008) hard-checks customer_id = auth.uid(), so a lone cleaner in a stranger's home has no recourse or paper trail. One-sided safety reporting is a defining T&S gap for in-home services.

roadmap.name: Live cleaner-map matching experience (Finding your cleaner)
roadmap.whatItIs: A full-bleed takeover state of /bookings/[id] while broadcasting/accepted/no_cleaner_found: a real map centered on the town, fuzzed pins for every real booking_offers row, live N-notified and M-deciding counts, a radar sweep, and a winner reveal that locks one pin and slides up the confirmed-cleaner card. Presentation layer over existing dispatch, no fake GPS or ETA. Migration 0009, one fuzzing SECURITY DEFINER RPC, about 5 components.
roadmap.why: The founder's explicit ask, and the Uber feel here is genuinely real: request_booking truly broadcasts to every verified cleaner, first-accept-wins is truly the a-driver-accepted moment, and the counts are truly true. Suspense comes from motion plus live status, not invented telemetry. Full spec in docs/specs/uber-cleaner-map.md.
roadmap.dependencies: soft-depends on the P0 scheduler for the honest broadcast timeout; migration 0009 (geo/centroids/RPC)

roadmap.name: Live dispatch transparency counts (map-optional)
roadmap.whatItIs: The notified/deciding/still-searching status from count(booking_offers) plus offer-state transitions, delivered through the same get_booking_matching RPC and degrading to a text-only card when the map cannot load or supply is thin.
roadmap.why: Turns the anxious black-box Finding-a-cleaner wait into transparent, reassuring live status. Ships inside the flagship but lands its value even without tiles, on data already written by request_booking.
roadmap.dependencies: ships with the live cleaner-map (shares the RPC)

roadmap.name: Saved Homes (address book)
roadmap.whatItIs: New customer_addresses table (label, full_address, area, access notes, owner-only RLS); /book swaps the raw text field for pick-a-saved-home-or-add-new with save-on-first-use.
roadmap.why: Today /book is a raw text field plus manual area select; the address is only persisted per-booking. The single biggest repeat-friction remover and the unblocker for one-tap re-book and recurring.

roadmap.name: One-tap Re-book with prefill
roadmap.whatItIs: Make the existing Book Again CTA carry prior area/hours/address/add-ons via query params, or a clone-and-broadcast action for a new date.
roadmap.why: BOOK_AGAIN_ALLOWED already renders the CTA but it opens an empty /book; all the data to prefill is on the booking row. The natural I-want-what-I-had-last-time path.

roadmap.name: Wire up customer_favorites (toggle + favorites-first dispatch tier)
roadmap.whatItIs: Heart toggle on the post-job cleaner card, a Favorites list on /account, and an offer-my-favorites-first exclusive window before the area broadcast. Folds favorite cleaners, continuity booking, and the ops favorite-priority tier.
roadmap.why: customer_favorites exists with full SELECT/INSERT/DELETE RLS (0008) but has ZERO references in src/, a built, stranded backend. Pure broadcast has no continuity; the cheapest retention lever.
roadmap.dependencies: none (becomes a tier once ranked dispatch lands)

roadmap.name: Recurring / standing bookings + discount
roadmap.whatItIs: recurring_plans (frequency, weekday, time, hours, area, saved home, optional preferred cleaner); the scheduler auto-creates and broadcasts the next occurrence; optional recurring discount.
roadmap.why: Everything today is a one-off; recurring is the single biggest LTV lever for a cleaning marketplace and reuses request_booking per occurrence.

roadmap.name: Add-on services / service tiers
roadmap.whatItIs: services plus booking_addons catalog (deep clean, oven, fridge, windows, move-out, laundry) with price/duration; /book checkboxes; fold into request_booking total/deposit/balance; itemized payment card.
roadmap.why: Pricing is flat rate times hours today. Add-ons raise average order value and clarity, and feed per-service SEO pages. Merges Customer add-ons and Monetization service tiers.

roadmap.name: On my way + ETA status (no map)
roadmap.whatItIs: Day-of cleaner action sets en_route plus ETA; customer sees Maria-is-on-her-way and a notification.
roadmap.why: Reuses StatusLive realtime plus notifications; delivers about 80% of the live-tracking value with zero map/GPS. Top-requested for on-site services.

roadmap.name: Special instructions + entry/access details
roadmap.whatItIs: notes plus access_instructions on the booking, masked until deposit via the proven booking_addresses RLS pattern.
roadmap.why: The book form has no notes field; the codebase already has a masked-until-deposit pattern to copy. Avoids day-of phone tag and lockouts.

roadmap.name: Tipping
roadmap.whatItIs: New tip payment_type plus a tip step on the review screen (10/15/20%/custom) reusing the Stripe Checkout plus webhook flow; 100% to the cleaner, shown in earnings.
roadmap.why: payment_type is an enum and the Checkout plus idempotent-webhook flow is already built and reusable; the review prompt is the natural post-job moment. A tip-able platform attracts better supply.

roadmap.name: Receipts & booking summary (view + emailed)
roadmap.whatItIs: Itemized per-booking receipt (service, hours, cleaner, deposit plus balance plus tip), printable, emailed on balance_paid.
roadmap.why: All data lives in payments plus bookings; the webhook already fires on payment completion. Additive read layer that builds trust in a new brand.

roadmap.name: Promo codes & first-clean intro offer
roadmap.whatItIs: MVP: allow_promotion_codes on Checkout. Fuller: promo_codes validated in request_booking with a launch FIRST10.
roadmap.why: No discount primitive exists, but Stripe Checkout supports promo codes natively, so the MVP is nearly free. Essential to kick-start a brand-new town.

roadmap.name: Account credit / wallet ledger (enabler)
roadmap.whatItIs: account_credits ledger applied as the first discount line in request_booking/checkout; admins can grant goodwill credit.
roadmap.why: Referrals, gift cards, win-back, and dispute goodwill all need somewhere to deposit value. Build the ledger once instead of three bespoke implementations.

roadmap.name: Referral credits (give-get)
roadmap.whatItIs: Per-customer code; both sides get CAD credit on the referee's first paid job; share card on /account.
roadmap.why: In a tight-knit Comox Valley launch, word-of-mouth is the cheapest CAC; the credit-apply step is a localized change in request_booking pricing math.

roadmap.name: Ranked tiered offer dispatch (replace pure broadcast)
roadmap.whatItIs: Score eligible cleaners (proximity, rating, acceptance_rate, responsiveness, fairness, favorites) and release offers in waves; keep accept_offer atomic first-wins. Add dispatch_tier/offered_at to booking_offers.
roadmap.why: request_booking is a flat ring-everyone insert with no ordering, creating a thundering-herd race that punishes thoughtful cleaners. The ops centerpiece and the mechanism behind every priority perk; the map will visually expose how few cleaners actually respond.
roadmap.dependencies: P0 scheduler, cleaner stats, cleaner geo

roadmap.name: Cleaner performance/reliability stats
roadmap.whatItIs: cleaner_stats maintained by triggers: offers received/accepted, acceptance_rate, avg_response_seconds, completed, cancellations, no_shows, avg_rating, last_assigned_at.
roadmap.why: Acceptance-rate ranking is requested but untracked, and rating is recomputed on the fly. One table feeds ranked dispatch, dashboards, auto-suspend, and fraud signals with O(1) reads.

roadmap.name: Time-aware availability schedule
roadmap.whatItIs: cleaner_availability (weekday windows) plus blackout dates; dispatch only rings cleaners whose schedule covers scheduled_at.
roadmap.why: request_booking rings every active plus verified cleaner regardless of whether they can work then, a guaranteed wall of declines and alert fatigue the read-only availability_note already hinted at.

roadmap.name: Stripe Connect Express payouts + payout ledger
roadmap.whatItIs: Onboard cleaners to Connect Express; attach application_fee_amount plus transfer_data.destination to deposit/balance Checkout so the take-rate splits at source; a real Payouts tab.
roadmap.why: The earnings page promises weekly direct deposit and a 15% fee with no money-movement behind it. Reliable, transparent payouts are the number-one reason supply stays. Stripe is already the rail, so Connect is the natural extension.

roadmap.name: Before/after photo proof of completion
roadmap.whatItIs: Supabase Storage job-photos bucket plus gate complete_job on at least one after-photo; gallery on the booking page above Pay balance.
roadmap.why: complete_job just sets status='completed' with zero evidence, yet the balance is charged on trust. The biggest evidence gap on both sides for an in-home clean.

roadmap.name: Customer sign-off gate + auto-confirm before balance
roadmap.whatItIs: A Confirm-the-job-is-done step (tied to after-photos); balance payable only on confirm; 48h silence auto-confirms via cron; reject routes to re-clean/dispute.
roadmap.why: Today completed leads to a Pay-balance button with no acceptance step and no timeout, so an unhappy customer just stalls. A time-boxed sign-off de-risks both sides.

roadmap.name: Live ops dispatch board (/admin/dispatch)
roadmap.whatItIs: Realtime view of every in-flight booking (tier, counts, deposit countdown, SLA flags) plus manual force-rebroadcast / assign / extend / cancel.
roadmap.why: The admin dashboard is static counts only. Realtime plus admin override RLS already exist, so a dispatcher could steer and rescue stuck jobs from one screen.

roadmap.name: Append-only audit log
roadmap.whatItIs: audit_log written by triggers plus every admin server action (verification, reassign, refund, settings, dispute) with actor and before/after JSON.
roadmap.why: There is zero audit trail for the many SECURITY DEFINER state changes and admin overrides. Backbone for dispute resolution, chargeback evidence, fraud review, and the support console.

roadmap.name: Programmatic per-area + per-service SEO landing pages
roadmap.whatItIs: /cleaning/[area] and /[service]/[area] via generateStaticParams over AREAS times services, localized copy, LocalBusiness JSON-LD (centroids known), live ratings, sitemap.
roadmap.why: For a geo-bounded local marketplace, organic local search is the highest-ROI growth channel, and App Router makes static generation trivial. Today there is one marketing page.

roadmap.name: Report & block users
roadmap.whatItIs: user_reports plus user_blocks; exclude blocked cleaners from a customer's future broadcasts in request_booking; admin review queue.
roadmap.why: No report/block primitive exists, and request_booking rings everyone with no exclusion list, so a bad/unsafe match can recur indefinitely.

roadmap.name: Get-directions deep link + tap-to-call
roadmap.whatItIs: Once deposit_paid unmasks the address, render a maps and tel link from the cleaner job page.
roadmap.why: The jobs page already reveals full_address at deposit_paid as static text. A maps URL needs zero map library, practical friction removal for cleaners driving between jobs.

roadmap.name: Off-peak / dynamic pricing
roadmap.whatItIs: pricing_rules (day/time multipliers plus optional supply-based surge keyed off the active-cleaner count dispatch already queries) applied in request_booking; Save-15%-weekday-mornings on /book.
roadmap.why: Flat pricing leaves demand-shaping money on the table; cleaners fill idle weekday mornings and peak demand is captured at a premium. The dispatch query already knows area coverage.

roadmap.name: Two-way reviews (cleaner rates customer)
roadmap.whatItIs: Add reviewer_id/reviewee_id plus double-blind reveal; cleaner review form on completion.
roadmap.why: Reviews are one-way and the schema is half-built; cleaners can flag difficult/unsafe customers and build mutual accountability.

roadmap.name: Abandoned-deposit recovery + appointment reminders
roadmap.whatItIs: Scheduler scans accepted-but-unpaid bookings to send reminder, incentive, then expire/re-broadcast at deposit_deadline; and scans upcoming bookings for day-before/morning-of confirmations.
roadmap.why: Accepted-but-unpaid bookings are the closest-to-converting revenue and are abandoned silently today; reminders cut lockouts. deposit_deadline already exists, just unenforced.

roadmap.name: no_cleaner_found recovery flow
roadmap.whatItIs: Instead of dead-ending: alert admin, optionally widen to adjacent towns, and offer try-another-time / notify-me / broaden-area.
roadmap.why: request_booking sets no_cleaner_found and stops, nothing notifies or retries. A failed match becomes recoverable instead of a lost customer; plugs into the map fallback.

roadmap.name: Earnings analytics, ratings dashboard & onboarding checklist
roadmap.whatItIs: Inline-SVG earnings trend bars plus avg per-job; a cleaner ratings panel (avg, distribution, recent comments); and a guided onboarding completeness card gating dispatch eligibility.
roadmap.why: The data already exists (earnings computes totals; reviews are public) but the cleaner UI never visualizes trends or shows reviews, and onboarding is a single areas checkbox then a silent wait. Pure read-side supply retention, no new deps.

roadmap.name: Cleaner approximate geo (opt-in base point)
roadmap.whatItIs: Optional cleaner_details.approx_lat/lng (coarse, about 1km snapped, captured at onboarding), never exposed raw, always fuzzed at query time.
roadmap.why: Anchors map pins and proximity ranking near reality instead of pure centroid jitter. v1 of the map does not need it, but it improves the flagship and unblocks distance-based dispatch.

roadmap.name: Booking progress timeline (stepper)
roadmap.whatItIs: Requested then Matched then Deposit then In progress then Done then Paid, derived from booking.status.
roadmap.why: STATUS_LABEL/COLOR maps already exist; a pure presentational upgrade that reduces what-now confusion with no backend work.

roadmap.name: Gift cards + lapsed-customer win-back
roadmap.whatItIs: Stripe Checkout gift cards that load the wallet (/gift), and a scheduler job that finds N-day-lapsed customers and sends a comeback promo plus one-tap rebook.
roadmap.why: Recipients are zero-CAC new users and reactivation is far cheaper than acquisition; both reuse the Checkout pattern, wallet, and lifecycle email.
roadmap.dependencies: wallet ledger, P0 email, promo codes

roadmap.name: Satisfaction guarantee / free re-clean + guarantee page
roadmap.whatItIs: Within a window, a linked zero-dollar follow-up to the same cleaner before any refund, plus a Dust Busters Guarantee policy page and structured property_damage claim.
roadmap.why: The deposit screen promises work to-your-satisfaction with no mechanism behind it; turns would-be refunds/disputes into fixed cleans and sets honest coverage expectations.

roadmap.name: Cleaner profile photo + bio, calendar view, tax/CSV exports
roadmap.whatItIs: Avatar (Storage) plus bio surfaced in get_cleaner_card; a week/agenda job view with conflict highlighting; per-year cleaner income CSV plus admin bookings/payments/disputes exports with BC/GST summary.
roadmap.why: Identity helps win acceptances and feeds favoriting; the agenda data is already calendar-ready; CAD-contractor tax exports are a one-route stream off existing rollups. Professional polish, not blockers.

roadmap.name: Memberships & cleaner Pro subscription
roadmap.whatItIs: Dust Busters Plus (discounted rate, waived fee, free reschedules, dispatch head-start) and a cleaner Pro subscription for an earlier offer window plus badge.
roadmap.why: Two recurring-revenue streams that reuse the ranked-dispatch timing mechanism and Stripe subscriptions; keep boosts bounded so quality is never overridden. Defer until ranked dispatch plus Connect exist.

roadmap.name: Web push for cleaners + fraud rules engine
roadmap.whatItIs: Service worker plus VAPID push so offers reach cleaners with the app closed; and a rule-based risk scorer (same-person book/cancel, off-platform leakage, accept-then-decline blocking, no-show streaks) into an admin risk queue.
roadmap.why: First-accept-wins makes an unseen offer lost income (P0 SMS covers most of this sooner), and a two-sided deposit marketplace has clear off-platform-leakage incentives none of which are detected today. Heavier, post-scale bets.
roadmap.dependencies: P0 email/SMS (push), audit log plus cleaner stats (fraud)

recommendedNow: P0.1 Fix the reviews reviewer_id bug (trivial) - restores a broken correctness check and unblocks two-way reviews
P0.3 Schedule-conflict / double-booking guard in accept_offer and request_booking (small) - stops a guaranteed-no-show bug
P0.4 Cleaner self-serve Online/Offline toggle (small) - one-line dispatch filter, supply table-stakes
P0.2 Dispatch scheduler heartbeat (medium) - offer expiry plus deposit_deadline enforcement plus broadcast TTL; also powers the map's honest timeout
P0.5 Enforced cancellation windows plus automatic Stripe refunds (medium) - removes chargeback/legal exposure on a policy the UI already promises
P0.6 Commission-in-data plus honest payout/fee copy (medium) - make the marketplace actually record revenue and stop the false Friday-direct-deposit claim
P0.8 Transactional email on money/match events (medium) - cash collection depends on off-site deposit/balance nudges
P0.7 Real ID verification: document upload plus admin review (medium) - make the ID-verified/background-checked claim true
P0.9 Cleaner-side issue reporting (small) - lone-worker safety; generalize open_dispute
P1 Live cleaner-map matching plus transparency counts (medium) - the flagship; migration 0009 plus about 5 components per docs/specs/uber-cleaner-map.md
Fast follows that amplify the map and are nearly free: Saved Homes (small), One-tap Re-book (trivial), Get-directions deep link (trivial), no_cleaner_found recovery (small)
synthesis.mapVerdict: Build it now, but ship the honest broadcast-theatre version, not faked GPS. Scope: a full-bleed takeover state of the existing /bookings/[id] page (no route changes) shown while status is broadcasting/accepted/no_cleaner_found. It is a pure presentation layer over the dispatch engine you already shipped, every pin is a real booking_offers row, every count is real, the winner is a real accept_offer. Library: react-leaflet 5 plus leaflet 1.9 plus OSM raster tiles as the primary interactive basemap (zero cost, no key, React-19-ready, isolated behind dynamic with ssr false), with a stylized static SVG basemap as the reduced-motion/tile-failure/thin-supply fallback, both consuming the identical center-and-pins contract. Privacy: one SECURITY DEFINER RPC, get_booking_matching, is the ONLY data path (customers cannot read booking_offers under RLS, and must never be granted it). It returns town centroid, fuzzed pins with opaque tokens, the notified/deciding counts, an expiry timestamp, and a safe winner card, never a cleaner_id, real coordinate, or address. Fuzz pins deterministically seeded by cleaner_id concatenated with booking_id (stable within a search, uncorrelated across bookings, so cleaners cannot be triangulated); because positions are synthesized there is no real location to recover. Realtime: reuse the StatusLive postgres_changes pattern for the load-bearing winner/terminal transition (push, instant) plus an approximately 4-second poll of the RPC for the live deciding decrement (v1; pg_notify in v2). Honesty contract is non-negotiable: no moving pins, no fake ETA (show the scheduled slot), no phantom cleaners, no SLA countdown (count up). Migration 0009 adds centroids table, broadcast_expires_at, settings.broadcast_ttl_mins, fuzz_pin, get_booking_matching, expiry via the P0 scheduler. I reconciled the UX design and tech plan into one buildable spec at docs/specs/uber-cleaner-map.md: I took the tech plan's concrete leaflet plus OSM and divIcon path and SSR risk patterns, the UX design's richer counts plus winner RPC and (cleaner_id, booking_id) fuzz seed, and merged both edge-case tables. Effort is medium (roughly 3 to 5 days) and best sequenced right after the P0 scheduler so its timeout is truthful.
